_about: Measured ground truth for the SR6 (ORS6) mechanism, extracted directly from the 30 official TempestMAx SR6 STL parts (measured/measure.py) and the SR6-Alpha4_ESP32.ino firmware. These are the ANCHORS the kinematic model (../true_kinematics.py) is built on. No guessed coordinates.
source_stls: official SR6 STL set (SR6测试版零件 / SR6 T-wist4 / 备用零件), 30 parts
source_firmware: SR6-Alpha4_ESP32.ino (SetMainServo L837-844, SetPitchServo L851-862, main mix L765-771)

link_lengths_mm._rod_note: MainLink STL Y-extent 191mm (ball material included); eye-to-eye 175mm == firmware sqrt(28125+2500). PitchLink shares the 175 rod plus a 55mm@15deg pitcher offset arm (firmware pitchOff=5500/100=55, pitchAng=0.2618rad); PitchLink X-extent 64mm confirms the offset arm.

receiver.frame_note: Receiver STL print-frame: ring axis ~Z, X is left/right (side), main attach lugs at |X|~70 ears, pitch attach at the rear. Mapped into the control/kinematic frame the model uses main pivots at X=+-60 and pitch pivots at X=+-61.

servo_axes.note: Main arm (SR6 臂 STL) is a planar part (hub and tip share one X); mounted on an X-parallel servo shaft the arm tip stays in that X-plane. For the firmware's 2D planar IK to hold at home, the servo shaft must lie in the SAME X-plane as its receiver ball: X=+-60 (main), X=+-61 (pitch). This is why the model places servos in-plane, NOT at the previously-guessed X=+-85.

home._home_note: HOME_H - 162.48 = 30.52; firmware home SetMainServo(16248,1500) => in-plane (vertical 162.48mm, horizontal 15mm). At home every leg's out-of-plane offset h=0 => all 6 rods are EXACTLY 175.000mm.

closure_result.interpretation: In-plane motions (thrust/fwd/pitch-rotation) keep h=0 so firmware 2D-IK is EXACT (gap 0). Only genuinely out-of-plane motions (side, roll) move pivots off their plane by h, giving the irreducible firmware-vs-rigid gap sqrt(175^2+h^2)-175. Honest, physical, not a tautology, not a guessed-coordinate artifact.
